raw_consumer_statement:
Subject : American Express Violating FCBA - Misapplying 60-Day Rule & Falsely Closing Dispute Sequence of Events & Company Errors : XX/XX/XXXX : I submitted disputes for two charges from XXXX XXXX for a single failed XXXX procedure : {$1400.00} ( XX/XX/XXXX ) and {$480.00} ( XX/XX/XXXX ). 

Amex 's Initial Errors : Amex incorrectly labeled the {$1400.00} dispute as a " tort '' ( medical malpractice ) case and denied the {$480.00} dispute for being " over 60 days '' from the charge date. They ignored that my dispute was for defective services, where the 60-day period starts from defect discovery or merchant refusal, not the charge date. 

My Response : I repeatedly contacted Amex, explained this was a defective XXXX service completed on XX/XX/XXXX, with the defect discovered in XXXX and the merchant refusing repair on XX/XX/XXXX. I requested they apply the correct Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) rule. 

Amex 's Final Errors ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : Amex closed both disputes, reiterating the wrong XXXX reason. Crucially, they closed the {$1400.00} dispute with a note stating " you requested we cancel the claim, '' which is FALSE. I never requested cancellation ; I requested escalation and correction. 

Legal Violation : American Express is violating the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), Section 161 ( a ) ( 3 ). For disputes regarding " services not delivered as agreed '' ( defective services ), the 60-day period to notify the bank begins when the defect is discovered or when the merchant refuses to fix itnot from the original charge date. My dispute is within 60 days of XX/XX/XXXX ( the date XXXX XXXX refused repair ).
